---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
##xaringan::inf_mr()
## For offline work: https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/some-tips.html#working-offline
## Images not appearing? Put images folder inside the libs folder as that is the main data directory

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-worldmap3.png')
background-size: 105%
background-class: top

<br>

.size60[

**Today's Agenda: Final Paper Prep**

- Reviewing international political events
]

<br>

.center[.size40[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2022)
]]

???


## Prep for class
1. Review submitted cases and citations

<br>




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_triangles2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size50[.content-box-purple[**Final Exam (Due Dec 12, 10am)**]]

.size35[
Select a **NEW** international political event and make an argument that one of the IR theories / models we've studied in class this term "best" explains the event. 

Your paper must consider and evaluate how **THREE** of the IR theories from class explain your chosen event.

- No double-dipping on realism/liberalism

- At least one theory from the second half of class

- No events used in the prior paper
]

???

### Questions on the assignment?




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_triangles.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size55[
.content-box-white[**For Today:**]

1. Find a NEW international political event you want to focus on for the final paper (no overlap), and

2. Submit at least **TWO** pieces of evidence that help us understand what happened.
]

???

Our aim for today is to make sure everyone has an international political event picked out and ready to analyze in the paper.

<br>

### Did everybody submit their case and evidence?

<br>

*Split class into groups of four*

- Go sit with your group!




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_triangles.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size55[
.content-box-white[**Present Each Event**]

1. Is this an international political event?

2. Is the evidence provided sufficiently high quality to establish the key facts?
]

???

SMALL GROUPS: Discuss each case and give the presenter feedback on these two points.

<br>

### Questions?

- Get to it!

<br>

Alright, let's hear all the cases you've picked!




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_triangles.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size50[
.content-box-white[**For Next Class:**]

1. Submit a brief description of your chosen case, and

2. Briefly apply each of your three chosen models to the case and explain **the strengths and the weaknesses** of each model when explaining your event.
]

???

### Questions on the assignment?
